nodes.label: Error Rates ↓100x
nodes.description: Physical error rates must decrease by two orders of magnitude to enable fault-tolerant quantum computing. Current best: ~10⁻³, needed: ~10⁻⁵.

nodes.label: Qubit Count ↑1000x
nodes.description: Scaling from ~1,000 physical qubits to millions. Requires solving wiring, crosstalk, and yield challenges simultaneously.

nodes.label: Coherence ↑100x
nodes.description: Qubit coherence times must increase ~100x to allow meaningful computation before quantum state decays.

nodes.label: Connectivity ↑10x
nodes.description: Most architectures have nearest-neighbor connectivity. Full or near-full connectivity is needed for efficient algorithms.

nodes.label: Cryogenics Scale
nodes.description: Dilution refrigerators must scale to cool millions of qubits at millikelvin temperatures. Current systems support ~1,000 qubits.

nodes.label: New Algorithms
nodes.description: Only a handful of quantum algorithms show theoretical speedup. Commercially relevant algorithms beyond Shor's and Grover's remain elusive.

nodes.label: Cost ↓10⁶x
nodes.description: Cost per useful quantum operation must decrease by ~6 orders of magnitude to compete with classical computing economics.

nodes.label: Error Correction
nodes.description: Quantum error correction requires 1,000-10,000 physical qubits per logical qubit. Overhead must decrease dramatically.

nodes.label: Control Electronics
nodes.description: Each qubit needs precise microwave control. Scaling to millions requires integrated cryogenic electronics that don't generate excess heat.

nodes.label: Software/Compilers
nodes.description: Quantum compilers and software stacks are the most mature area, but still far from production-ready for fault-tolerant systems.

nodes.label:
Commercial
Viability
nodes.description: Commercially viable quantum computing requires ALL breakthroughs to reach 100%. Partial progress yields zero commercial value.

coupling_edges.label: More qubits need more control lines
Decoherence is a source of errors
EC requires many more physical qubits
